title: Allow podman load to import multiple named refs from an OCI archive
body:
## Feature request description

I would like `podman load` to support an OCI archive that contains multiple names for the same image.

In practical terms, I want one OCI archive to be able to restore both of these local image references:

```text
localhost/ko-layout-smoke:latest
localhost/ko-layout-smoke:stable
```

where both tags point to the same image or image index.

Today, Podman rejects this kind of archive.

## What I Tested

I created an OCI archive whose top-level `index.json` contains two entries. Both entries point to the same image index digest. The only meaningful difference is the image name annotation.

Simplified `index.json`:

```json
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "manifests": [
    {
      "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json",
      "digest": "sha256:...",
      "size": 1234,
      "annotations": {
        "org.opencontainers.image.ref.name": "localhost/ko-layout-smoke:latest"
      }
    },
    {
      "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json",
      "digest": "sha256:...",
      "size": 1234,
      "annotations": {
        "org.opencontainers.image.ref.name": "localhost/ko-layout-smoke:stable"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Plain-language interpretation:

```text
There is one underlying image/index at sha256:...
Please import it as localhost/ko-layout-smoke:latest
Please also import it as localhost/ko-layout-smoke:stable
```

## Current Podman Behavior

`podman load -i layout-multi-ref.tar` rejects the archive:

```text
Error: unable to load image: payload does not match any of the supported image formats:
 * oci: open /var/tmp/libpod-images-load.tar3956958393/index.json: not a directory
 * oci-archive: loading index: more than one image in oci, choose an image
 * docker-archive: loading tar component "manifest.json": file does not exist
 * dir: reading version file "/var/tmp/libpod-images-load.tar3956958393/version": open /var/tmp/libpod-images-load.tar3956958393/version: not a directory
```

## Behavior In kind / ctr

The same archive works with kind v0.32.0 through `kind load image-archive`. Kind streams the archive into the node with `ctr images import`.

After import, both tags exist and point to the same image ID:

```text
localhost/ko-layout-smoke  latest  83ac692030a19  2.09MB
localhost/ko-layout-smoke  stable  83ac692030a19  2.09MB
```

Pods using either tag with `imagePullPolicy=Never` completed successfully.

## Why This Seems Reasonable

An OCI layout can contain multiple top-level entries in `index.json`. Each entry can have an `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name` annotation. That annotation is the image name to use when importing.

For this use case, multiple entries do not mean multiple unrelated images. They can be multiple names for the same image digest, like Docker tags.

`ctr images import` handles this by reading each top-level `index.json` entry and creating image records from the name annotations.

Relevant source references:

- kind streams image archives to `ctr images import --all-platforms --digests`: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kind/blob/v0.32.0/pkg/cluster/nodeutils/util.go#L80-L90
- `ctr images import` creates an import stream and transfers it to an image store: https://github.com/containerd/containerd/blob/9e70782d9a0e92900f402b2c7a4e2aa30754503c/cmd/ctr/commands/images/import.go#L188-L193
- the transfer importer loops over each top-level `index.json` entry and marks it as nameable from annotations: https://github.com/containerd/containerd/blob/9e70782d9a0e92900f402b2c7a4e2aa30754503c/core/transfer/local/import.go#L79-L83
- the transfer importer stores each descriptor: https://github.com/containerd/containerd/blob/9e70782d9a0e92900f402b2c7a4e2aa30754503c/core/transfer/local/import.go#L131-L134
- the image store creates an image record from the annotation-derived name: https://github.com/containerd/containerd/blob/9e70782d9a0e92900f402b2c7a4e2aa30754503c/core/transfer/image/imagestore.go#L236-L267
- the name is read from `io.containerd.image.name` first, then `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name`: https://github.com/containerd/containerd/blob/9e70782d9a0e92900f402b2c7a4e2aa30754503c/core/transfer/image/imagestore.go#L480-L498

## Suggest potential solution

Allow `podman load` to import OCI archives that contain multiple top-level entries when those entries have usable image-name annotations.

A conservative first step could support only the case where:

- each top-level entry has `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name`
- repeated digests are allowed
- multiple names can point to the same digest
- ambiguous entries without image-name annotations can still be rejected

That would allow one OCI archive to preserve multiple tags for the same image or image index.

## Have you considered any alternatives?

The current workaround is to produce one OCI archive per tag:

```sh
podman save --format oci-archive -o app-v1.oci.tar localhost/app:v1
podman save --format oci-archive -o app-latest.oci.tar localhost/app:latest
```

That works, but duplicates the archive artifact and loses the natural relationship that `v1` and `latest` are just two names for the same image/index.

Another workaround is Docker archive format, but that is not ideal for workflows that specifically want OCI archive semantics, especially for multi-platform image-index workflows.

## Additional context

This came up while evaluating `oci-archive` output for ko and GoReleaser. The goal is a Docker-free and registry-free CI workflow where a multi-platform image index is built as a single portable OCI archive, scanned, loaded into kind for tests, and later published to an OCI registry.

Related docs:

- Podman documents `podman save --format oci-archive` as producing "a tar archive using the OCI Image Format": https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-save.1.html#format-format
- Podman documents `podman load` as loading local tar archives: https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-load.1.html#input-i-input

Tested with Podman v6.0.1 and kind v0.32.0 on darwin/arm64.

comments.body: This seems to be a good idea. WDYT? @podman-container-tools/podman-maintainers
there is/was https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1178 for multiple image archives, I guess this just needs someone to finish the work
@mtrmac Can you remember where it got stuck, or where the problematic part is?

I think it was just the original contributor leaving / having other priorities, no specific strong blocker; the whole concept is proven by the feature existing in `docker-archive:`.

I do think this is a good idea and a valuable feature (though I have little opinion on relative priority). _Right now_ I’d recommend waiting with this work for 2-3 weeks because some other work needs to land first, and there would be fairly significant merge conflicts.

---

On a higher level, I would strongly recommend using a registry to transport images. It has, by necessity, support for concurrent accesses, the full feature set, and layer deduplication / reuse. Whereas any tar-based workflows are going to copy the whole images every time, and e.g. signatures are unsupported in `docker-archive:` and unimplemented in `oci:`/`oci-archive:`.
